wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Palindrome game project overview. Explain the core concept of this cross-platform word-based puzzle game featuring palindrome creation mechanics with multiplayer capabilities. Document the technology stack including React Native 0.81.5, Expo 54.0.31, Supabase integration, and modern architecture patterns. Describe the target audience, key features including single-player gameplay, real-time multiplayer matches, friend system, and authentication. Include project structure overview, development workflow, and platform support (iOS, Android, Web). Provide context about the game's educational value in language skills and cognitive development. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and make content accessible to both beginners and experienced developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Palindrome Detection Algorithm
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the palindrome detection algorithm implementation. Explain the core logic for identifying palindromes in both row and column directions through the placed tile position. Document the checkPalindromes function including line extraction, palindrome validation using array reversal, and scoring calculation. Detail the algorithm's approach to finding the longest continuous segment containing the new tile, handling edge cases, and minimum length requirements. Include examples of palindrome detection scenarios, performance characteristics, and optimization techniques. Address the integration with bulldog bonus calculation and segment length reporting for UI feedback.

wiki_catalogs.name: Matchmaking Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the matchmaking service implementation. Document the quick match creation and joining mechanisms using atomic operations and PostgreSQL functions. Explain the invite-based private match system with unique 6-character invite codes and collision handling. Detail the match lifecycle from creation through active gameplay to completion. Cover the claim_quick_match RPC function for race-condition prevention and the atomic match claiming process. Document match status management (waiting, active, finished, cancelled) and the associated state transitions. Include player management operations, seed generation for game initialization, and time limit configuration. Provide practical examples of match creation workflows, error handling strategies, and performance considerations for concurrent match requests.

wiki_catalogs.name: Scoring Algorithm
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the scoring algorithm implementation. Document the palindrome detection logic including row and column scanning, segment extraction, and palindrome validation. Explain the scoring calculation formula with base segment length scoring and bulldog bonus mechanics. Detail the segment length tracking for UI feedback (GOOD for 3-length, GREAT for 4-length, etc.). Include examples of various scoring scenarios showing different segment lengths, bulldog position effects, and cumulative scoring. Document the checkPalindromes function parameters, return values, and performance characteristics. Address edge cases like minimum length requirements and segment boundary detection.

wiki_catalogs.name: Game State Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the GameState interface and related data structures. Explain the Grid type definition as a 2D array of numbers or null values representing the game board. Document the GameState interface properties including grid, blockCounts array, score, bulldogPositions array, and moveCount. Detail the ScoringResult interface for move validation feedback and HintMove interface for hint generation. Include examples of state initialization, state property relationships, and data type constraints. Provide code examples showing proper state construction and property access patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive system architecture documentation for the Palindrome game. Describe the overall system design including React Native with Expo Router framework, cross-platform architecture, and component hierarchy. Document the provider pattern implementation with ThemeProvider, SettingsProvider, and Auth context. Explain the routing architecture using Expo Router with tab navigation and protected routes. Detail the separation of concerns between presentation layer, business logic, and data persistence. Include system boundaries, external dependencies (Supabase, Google Sign-In), and integration patterns. Address platform-specific adaptations for iOS, Android, and Web. Document the global state management approach and how components interact through context providers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the Palindrome game development environment. Include detailed installation instructions for Node.js, npm, and Expo CLI. Document environment setup including .env configuration with SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY variables. Provide step-by-step setup for development tools including Android Studio, Xcode, and Expo Go. Explain the project initialization process, dependency installation, and first run procedures. Include platform-specific setup instructions for iOS and Android development environments. Document common development workflows, debugging techniques, and troubleshooting initial setup issues. Provide guidance on resetting the project if needed and understanding the file-based routing system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Game State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the game state management system. Detail the GameState interface structure including grid representation, block counts, score tracking, bulldog positions, and move counters. Explain state initialization through createInitialState function, state mutation patterns, and immutable state updates. Document the state transition logic in applyMove function, including validation, grid updates, and score accumulation. Cover state persistence considerations, serialization requirements for multiplayer synchronization, and state restoration mechanisms. Include examples of state snapshots, state comparison techniques, and debugging approaches for state consistency.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Communication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the real-time communication system using Supabase Realtime. Document the subscription mechanisms for match and match_players table changes, including channel creation and event filtering. Explain the live score synchronization system that updates opponent displays in real-time during gameplay. Detail the atomic score update operations and the conditions for score updates (submitted_at null check). Cover the subscription cleanup and channel removal patterns to prevent memory leaks. Document the fallback polling mechanism for rematch request updates and reliability strategies for network interruptions. Include examples of setting up subscriptions, handling connection events, and managing subscription lifecycles. Address performance considerations for multiple concurrent subscriptions and best practices for real-time data consistency.

wiki_catalogs.name: Inventory Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the block inventory system. Document the block counting mechanism with default counts per color, resource depletion during gameplay, and inventory state management. Explain the color-based inventory tracking system and how block consumption affects available moves. Detail the inventory initialization in createInitialState, block deduction in applyMove, and inventory validation in canPlace. Include examples of inventory state transitions, color distribution mechanics, and resource scarcity scenarios. Document the DEFAULT_BLOCK_COUNTS constant, NUM_COLORS configuration, and inventory persistence patterns. Address inventory optimization strategies and integration with game progression systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Initialization Process
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the createInitialState function and deterministic game setup. Explain the seed-based initialization system that ensures identical game states across platforms and multiplayer sessions. Document the bulldog position generation algorithm, blocked positions calculation for the PALINDROME word placement, and initial color distribution. Detail the random number generation using createSeededRandom, INITIAL_POSITIONS configuration, and default block count setup. Include examples of state snapshots at initialization, seed value impact on game outcomes, and debugging techniques for state verification.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed authentication architecture documentation for the Palindrome game system. Describe the authentication service implementation using Supabase Auth, multi-provider support (Google, Apple, Email/Password), and session management. Document the useAuth hook pattern, authentication state flow, and protected route handling. Explain the integration with Supabase client, token management, and user session persistence. Detail the authentication error handling strategy and user verification flow. Include the authentication context provider implementation and how authentication state propagates through the component tree. Address security considerations, token refresh mechanisms, and authentication state synchronization across platforms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Palindrome game system design. Describe the high-level architecture including React Native with Expo Router, Supabase backend integration, and cross-platform considerations. Document the provider pattern implementation for state management, service layer architecture for business logic, and component composition patterns. Explain the data flow from authentication through game state to multiplayer synchronization. Detail the separation of concerns between UI components, business logic services, and data persistence layers. Include system boundaries, integration points with external services, and architectural decisions rationale. Document the theme system, authentication flow, and real-time communication patterns. Address scalability considerations, performance optimization strategies, and cross-platform compatibility approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Move Validation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the move validation and execution system. Document the canPlace function for pre-validation checks including boundary validation, cell occupancy verification, and inventory availability. Explain the applyMove function's comprehensive validation pipeline and state mutation process. Detail the validation criteria for row/column placement, color availability, and game rules enforcement. Include the scoring result computation, state object immutability maintenance, and error handling strategies. Address performance optimization for validation checks, batch validation for AI systems, and validation caching mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Score Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the scoring system implementation. Document the live score updating mechanism that allows real-time score synchronization during gameplay using updateLiveScore function. Explain the final score submission process with submitScore function, including duplicate submission prevention and timing considerations. Detail the automatic winner determination logic based on score comparison and the is_winner flag assignment. Cover the match completion workflow triggered when both players submit their scores. Document the score validation, timestamp management, and the relationship between live updates and final submissions. Include examples of score update sequences, error handling for invalid operations, and edge cases like abandoned matches or player timeouts. Address the atomic nature of score operations and data consistency guarantees.

wiki_catalogs.name: Move Validation Mechanisms
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for move validation and state mutation functions. Document the applyMove function including input validation, grid mutation patterns, and state immutability principles. Explain the canPlace function for pre-validation checks, findScoringMove function for hint generation, and validation criteria for row/column boundaries, cell availability, and color inventory. Detail the state transition logic, new grid creation patterns, and block count management. Include examples of successful and failed move attempts, validation error scenarios, and performance considerations for real-time validation.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive state management architecture documentation for the Palindrome game. Describe the provider pattern implementation with ThemeProvider and SettingsProvider for global state management. Document the custom hooks architecture including useAuth, useColorScheme, and useThemeColor for localized state management. Explain the state flow from context providers to components, including theme switching, user preferences, and UI state management. Detail the separation between global application state and component-local state. Include state persistence strategies, state update patterns, and how different contexts interact. Address performance considerations, state optimization techniques, and debugging approaches for state-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive authentication system documentation for the Palindrome game. Detail the multi-provider authentication implementation supporting Google, Apple, and Email/Password authentication methods. Document the authentication service architecture, session management, and user profile handling. Explain the integration with Supabase Auth, token management, and secure storage mechanisms. Include authentication flow diagrams, error handling strategies, and user state management. Document the useAuth hook implementation, authentication guards, and protected route handling. Address security considerations, password policies, email verification processes, and account recovery procedures. Provide troubleshooting guides for common authentication issues and platform-specific authentication challenges.

wiki_catalogs.name: Hint Generation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the hint generation and solution discovery system. Document the findScoringMove function's algorithm for discovering valid moves that create scoring opportunities. Explain the brute-force search approach, candidate evaluation, and first-valid-move selection strategy. Detail the computational complexity considerations, optimization techniques for hint generation speed, and integration with user experience. Include examples of hint scenarios, hint difficulty levels, and the relationship between hint generation and game accessibility. Address performance tuning for real-time hint delivery and hint caching strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Rematch System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the rematch system implementation for post-match replay functionality. Explain the rematch request lifecycle from creation to acceptance, including the dual-click scenario where both players initiate rematches simultaneously. Detail the rematch_requests table structure and the pending/accepted/declined status management. Document the automatic match creation process when a rematch is accepted, including player addition and match initialization. Cover the request notification system and user interface integration for rematch prompts. Explain the relationship between original match completion and new match creation. Include examples of rematch workflows, request expiration handling, and the cleanup of resolved requests. Address the database constraints and foreign key relationships that maintain data integrity across rematch operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Scoring Algorithm
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the palindrome detection and scoring system. Explain the checkPalindromes function including row and column scanning algorithms, palindrome validation logic, and score calculation methodology. Document the MIN_PALINDROME_LENGTH constant, BULLDOG_BONUS scoring modifier, and segment length tracking for UI feedback. Detail the line scanning process, color array comparison, and bulldog position detection. Include examples of different palindrome lengths, scoring variations, and edge cases such as partial palindromes and bulldog bonus applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed data flow architecture documentation for the Palindrome game system. Describe the data flow patterns from user interactions through business logic services to persistence layers. Document the game state flow from gameEngine through UI components to Supabase real-time updates. Explain the multiplayer data synchronization patterns, including match state updates and real-time score synchronization. Detail the social data flow for friends system, challenges, and notifications. Include the data transformation pipeline, caching strategies, and offline-first approaches. Address error handling in data flows, data validation patterns, and consistency maintenance across distributed state updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Game Engine Core
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed game engine documentation for the Palindrome game mechanics. Document the core game logic including palindrome detection algorithms, grid-based tile placement, scoring systems, and hint generation. Explain the game state management, move validation, and turn-based mechanics. Detail the seeded random number generation for consistent puzzle creation, color-based tile representation, and inventory management. Include the algorithmic approaches for palindrome validation, word formation rules, and scoring calculations. Document the game engine's integration with UI components, state persistence, and real-time updates. Address performance optimization techniques, memory management, and cross-platform compatibility considerations for game logic execution.

wiki_catalogs.name: Random Number Generation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive content for the seeded random number generation system. Document the createSeededRandom function implementation using linear congruential generators for deterministic puzzle creation. Explain the seeding mechanism for consistent multiplayer experiences, state management for RNG instances, and cross-platform compatibility considerations. Detail the relationship between seed values and game board generation, reproducibility requirements for debugging, and entropy management. Include examples of seed usage patterns, random sequence generation, and integration with game initialization processes. Address performance characteristics and alternatives for different use cases.

wiki_catalogs.name: Match Data Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the multiplayer system entities. Document the Match interface with all properties including status enum values (waiting, active, finished, cancelled), time management fields, and match metadata. Detail the MatchPlayer interface covering player associations, scoring fields, submission timestamps, and winner status. Explain the RematchRequest interface for post-match replay requests including user relationships and status tracking. Document the database table relationships, foreign key constraints, and indexing strategies. Cover the data validation rules, default values, and business logic constraints. Include entity relationship diagrams showing match-player relationships and request-match associations. Address data lifecycle management, soft deletion strategies, and audit trail considerations for match history tracking.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Persistence and Synchronization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create documentation for state persistence and multiplayer synchronization requirements. Explain state serialization patterns for network transmission, state comparison techniques for detecting changes, and state restoration mechanisms. Document the immutable state update patterns, state snapshot creation for undo functionality, and state diff algorithms. Detail the relationship between state structure and multiplayer synchronization, including state reconciliation strategies and conflict resolution. Include examples of state persistence formats, synchronization protocols, and debugging approaches for state consistency issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive component architecture documentation for the Palindrome game UI system. Describe the component composition patterns, reusable UI components, and design system implementation. Document the themed components architecture including ThemedText and ThemedView for consistent theming. Explain the UI component hierarchy, prop patterns, and component composition strategies. Detail the platform-specific component adaptations for web and native platforms. Include the component lifecycle management, performance optimization techniques, and accessibility considerations. Address component testing strategies, prop validation patterns, and component documentation standards.

wiki_catalogs.name: Multiplayer System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive multiplayer system documentation for the Palindrome real-time gaming features. Document the matchmaking service implementation including quick match creation, invite-based private games, and player queue management. Explain the Supabase Realtime integration for live score synchronization, match state updates, and real-time communication. Detail the match lifecycle management from creation through completion, including player joining, game coordination, and result recording. Document the notification system for match invitations, status updates, and social interactions. Include the atomic operations for match creation, player management, and score updates. Address network reliability, offline handling, and synchronization strategies for distributed game state management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Scoring and Inventory System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the scoring calculation and inventory management system. Document the scoring algorithm including base segment scoring, bulldog bonus calculation, and maximum segment length tracking. Explain the block inventory system with color-based counting, resource management, and depletion mechanics. Detail the scoring result structure, segment length reporting for UI feedback, and score accumulation patterns. Include examples of scoring scenarios, bonus calculations, and inventory state changes. Address scoring optimization techniques, inventory persistence, and integration with game progression systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Social Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed social features documentation for the Palindrome friend and challenge system. Document the friend management implementation including friend requests, acceptance workflows, and friend list management. Explain the challenge system for head-to-head competitive play, match scheduling, and competition tracking. Detail the notification system architecture including real-time updates, user preferences, and notification delivery mechanisms. Include the database schema for social relationships, challenge records, and notification storage. Document the integration between social features and game mechanics, including friend leaderboard display and competitive statistics. Address privacy controls, social graph management, and scaling considerations for social feature performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Theme and UI System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive theme and UI system documentation for the Palindrome cross-platform interface. Document the ThemeContext implementation including color scheme management, theme switching, and platform-specific adaptations. Explain the component library architecture with reusable UI components, styling patterns, and responsive design considerations. Detail the dark/light theme implementation, accessibility features, and cross-platform UI consistency. Include the color system definition, typography hierarchy, and component composition patterns. Document the integration with React Native Paper, custom component development, and theme customization options. Address platform-specific UI adaptations for iOS, Android, and Web, including touch interactions, navigation patterns, and performance optimization for different form factors.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive database schema documentation for the Palindrome Supabase implementation. Document the complete table structure including profiles, matches, match_players, friends, challenges, and notifications tables. Detail the relationships between tables, foreign key constraints, and indexing strategies. Explain the row-level security policies for data isolation and access control. Include the migration management system, schema evolution patterns, and data integrity constraints. Document the Supabase configuration, authentication integration, and real-time subscription setup. Address data validation rules, business logic enforcement, and performance optimization through proper indexing and query patterns. Provide examples of common data access patterns and integration with the application's service layer.

wiki_catalogs.name: Platform-Specific Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed platform-specific features documentation for the Palindrome cross-platform development. Document the Android native integration including Gradle configuration, permissions, signing, and platform-specific optimizations. Explain the iOS integration through Xcode configuration, App Store requirements, and platform-specific features. Detail the web platform implementation including PWA capabilities, browser compatibility, and web-specific UI adaptations. Include the Expo configuration differences across platforms, native module integration, and platform-specific build processes. Document the responsive design patterns, touch vs. mouse interactions, and platform-specific performance considerations. Address platform-specific debugging techniques, testing strategies, and deployment considerations for each target platform.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build and Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive build and deployment documentation for the Palindrome application. Document the Expo Application Services (EAS) configuration including build profiles, environment variables, and automated build processes. Explain the native app build setup for Android APK/AAB and iOS IPA distribution. Detail the CI/CD pipeline implementation using GitHub Actions for automated testing and deployment. Include the release configuration, code signing processes, and distribution strategies for app stores and internal testing groups. Document the development build process, hot reloading capabilities, and debugging deployment issues. Address environment management, secrets handling, and production deployment best practices. Provide troubleshooting guides for common build failures and deployment problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing strategy documentation for the Palindrome application development. Document the testing approach including unit testing for game logic, integration testing for authentication and multiplayer features, and component testing for UI elements. Explain the TypeScript configuration for type safety in tests, ESLint rules for code quality, and VS Code integration for development workflow. Detail the testing frameworks used, mock strategies for Supabase integration, and testing patterns for asynchronous operations. Include testing guidelines for cross-platform compatibility, performance testing approaches, and accessibility testing procedures. Document the continuous integration testing pipeline, automated test execution, and quality assurance processes. Address testing best practices for React Native applications and debugging test failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting and FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting and FAQ documentation for the Palindrome development and deployment process. Document common development issues including environment setup problems, dependency conflicts, and platform-specific build errors. Include solutions for authentication configuration issues, Supabase connectivity problems, and multiplayer synchronization failures. wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Palindrome Game - Comprehensive Project Analysis

## Project Introduction

**Palindrome** is a sophisticated cross-platform mobile game built with React Native and Expo, featuring a unique word-based puzzle mechanics centered around creating palindromes on a grid. The game combines traditional puzzle gameplay with modern multiplayer features, social connectivity, and a polished user experience across iOS, Android, and web platforms.

### Core Goals and Objectives

- **Gameplay Innovation**: Implement a unique palindrome-based puzzle game with strategic depth
- **Cross-Platform Compatibility**: Deliver consistent experience across mobile and web platforms
- **Social Gaming**: Enable multiplayer matches, friend systems, and competitive play
- **Modern Architecture**: Utilize contemporary React Native patterns with TypeScript and Expo Router
- **Professional Quality**: Provide polished UI/UX with dark/light themes and smooth animations

### Target Audience

- Casual puzzle game enthusiasts seeking strategic challenges
- Competitive gamers interested in timed multiplayer matches
- Social gamers wanting to connect with friends and compete
- Cross-platform users requiring seamless experience across devices

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a modern React Native architecture with clear separation of concerns and platform abstraction layers.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[App Entry Point] --> B[Root Layout]
    B --> C[Theme Provider]
    B --> D[Auth Context]
    B --> E[Navigation Stack]
    
    E --> F[Tab Navigation]
    F --> G[Main Screen]
    F --> H[Multiplayer Screen]
    F --> I[Friends Screen]
    F --> J[Profile Screen]
    
    G --> K[Game Engine]
    H --> L[Matchmaking Service]
    I --> M[Friends Service]
    J --> N[Settings Context]
    
    K --> O[Shared Game Logic]
    L --> P[Realtime Updates]
    M --> Q[Social Features]
    N --> R[UI Preferences]
    
    O --> S[Supabase DB]
    P --> S
    Q --> S
    R --> T[Async Storage]
```

### Component Breakdown

The application is structured around several key architectural layers:

**Presentation Layer**: React components with themed styling and responsive layouts
**Business Logic Layer**: Game engine, matchmaking, and social features
**Data Layer**: Supabase integration for authentication, real-time data, and storage
**Infrastructure Layer**: Platform-specific configurations and native module integrations

### Design Patterns

- **Provider Pattern**: Centralized state management through React Context
- **Service Layer Pattern**: Modular business logic encapsulated in dedicated services
- **Repository Pattern**: Data access abstraction through Supabase client
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic component creation for game tiles and UI elements

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Application Bootstrap** (`app/_layout.tsx`)
- Global theme provider setup with gradient backgrounds
- Authentication state management and route protection
- Font loading and splash screen coordination
- Platform-specific navigation configuration

**Game Engine Core** (`lib/gameEngine.ts`)
- Centralized game logic for palindrome detection and scoring
- Deterministic state initialization for multiplayer synchronization
- Move validation and scoring calculation algorithms
- Hint system implementation for player assistance

### Core Modules

**Authentication Service** (`authService.ts`)
- Multi-provider authentication (Google, Apple, Email/Password)
- Session management with automatic refresh
- Profile management and caching strategies
- OAuth flow handling for web and native platforms

**Matchmaking System** (`lib/matchmaking.ts`)
- Real-time multiplayer match creation and management
- Invite code system for private matches
- Atomic quick match claiming to prevent race conditions
- Live score updates and match result processing

**Friends and Challenges** (`lib/friends.ts`)
- Friend request system with approval workflows
- Challenge system for competitive play
- Head-to-head statistics tracking
- User search and discovery features

**Notification System** (`lib/notifications.ts`)
- In-app notification management
- Real-time notification delivery
- Unread status tracking and persistence
- Custom notification data handling

### Configuration Approach

**Supabase Integration** (`supabase.ts`)
- Unified client configuration for all platforms
- Environment variable management
- Session persistence across reloads
- Platform-specific storage implementations

**Theme Management** (`context/ThemeContext.tsx`)
- Dark/light theme switching with persistent storage
- Gradient color schemes for visual consistency
- Responsive design adaptations
- Accessibility considerations

**Build Configuration** (`app.json`)
- Multi-platform build settings
- Native module configurations
- Asset optimization and bundling
- Platform-specific permissions and capabilities

### External Dependencies

**Core Framework Dependencies**:
- `@react-navigation/*`: Advanced routing and navigation
- `@supabase/supabase-js`: Real-time database and auth
- `expo-router`: File-based routing system
- `react-native-reanimated`: High-performance animations

**UI and Styling**:
- `expo-linear-gradient`: Beautiful gradient backgrounds
- `react-native-paper`: Material Design components
- `lucide-react`: Consistent iconography
- `react-native-spotlight-tour`: Interactive onboarding

**Platform Integrations**:
- `@react-native-google-signin/google-signin`: Google authentication
- `expo-apple-authentication`: Apple Sign-In support
- `expo-av`: Audio playback for game sounds
- `expo-blur`: Visual effects and overlays

### Integration Points

**Database Schema Evolution** (`supabase/migrations/`)
- Multiplayer tables with RLS policies
- Friend system and challenge tracking
- Notification infrastructure
- Atomic operations for fair matchmaking

**Real-time Communication**
- Postgres change events for live updates
- WebSocket connections for instant match updates
- Polling fallbacks for reliability
- Conflict resolution strategies

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Single Player Mode**
- Timed puzzle solving with progressive difficulty
- Hint system for guidance during gameplay
- Score tracking and personal bests
- Deterministic game states for replayability

**Multiplayer Features**
- Real-time competitive matches against random opponents
- Private invite-only games with custom codes
- Live score synchronization between players
- Match history and ranking systems

**Social Integration**
- Friend management with request/response system
- Challenge system for competitive play
- Head-to-head statistics and comparison
- Notification system for social interactions

**Progressive Onboarding**
- Interactive tutorial with guided walkthrough
- Step-by-step introduction to game mechanics
- Contextual help and tooltips
- Personalized user experience

### Implementation Highlights

**Game Engine Architecture** (`lib/gameEngine.ts`)
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Initializing
    Initializing --> Ready: Game State Created
    Ready --> Placing: Player Move
    Placing --> Validating: Move Submitted
    Validating --> Scoring: Palindrome Detected
    Validating --> Ready: Invalid Move
    Scoring --> Ready: Score Applied
    Ready --> [*]: Game Over
```

**Matchmaking Flow** (`lib/matchmaking.ts`)
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant P1 as Player 1
    participant MM as Matchmaking
    participant P2 as Player 2
    participant DB as Database
    
    P1->>+MM: Find Quick Match
    MM->>+DB: Claim Waiting Match
    DB-->>-MM: Match Found
    MM-->>-P1: Match Assigned
    
    P2->>+MM: Find Quick Match
    MM->>+DB: Create New Match
    DB-->>-MM: New Match
    MM-->>-P2: Match Assigned
    
    P1->>+DB: Update Live Score
    DB-->>-P1: Score Updated
    P2->>+DB: Update Live Score
    DB-->>-P2: Score Updated
```

**Social Features** (`lib/friends.ts`)
```mermaid
flowchart LR
    A[User Search] --> B[Friend Request]
    B --> C[Pending Approval]
    C --> D[Accepted Connection]
    D --> E[Challenge System]
    E --> F[Competitive Matches]
    
    G[Head-to-Head Stats] --> H[Win/Loss Tracking]
    H --> I[Historical Performance]
```

### Feature Architecture

**Responsive Design System**
- Adaptive layouts for different screen sizes
- Touch-friendly interface elements
- Platform-specific UI conventions
- Accessibility compliance

**Performance Optimizations**
- Memoized components for efficient rendering
- Lazy loading for non-critical resources
- Optimized animations and transitions
- Memory-efficient state management

**Cross-Platform Consistency**
- Shared business logic across platforms
- Platform abstraction for native features
- Unified styling system
- Consistent user experience

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [app.json](app.json)
- [app/_layout.tsx](app/_layout.tsx)
- [lib/gameEngine.ts](lib/gameEngine.ts)
- [context/ThemeContext.tsx](context/ThemeContext.tsx)
- [hooks/useAuth.ts](hooks/useAuth.ts)
- [authService.ts](authService.ts)
- [supabase.ts](supabase.ts)
- [app/(tabs)/main.tsx](app/(tabs)/main.tsx)
- [lib/matchmaking.ts](lib/matchmaking.ts)
- [lib/friends.ts](lib/friends.ts)
- [lib/notifications.ts](lib/notifications.ts)
- [constants/theme.ts](constants/theme.ts)

</blog>
